page: Microsoft Azure F4amds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Famdsv7 server family. Powered by an AMD processor and running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type includes 472 GB of local storage and is capable of utilizing Premium Storage. The combination of dedicated compute resources, a high-density memory configuration, and local disk capacity makes it a robust option for workloads requiring fast local read and write operations without relying solely on network-attached storage.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4amds_v7 offers a balanced resource density for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive CPU core counts. The inclusion of local storage and premium storage capabilities provides a qualitative advantage for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure F4amds_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famdsv7 family, powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs paired with 32.0 GB of memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance is bundled with 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, offering a balanced setup for data-intensive operations. Designed without a GPU, this server type is optimized for workloads that prioritize memory capacity and local disk performance over raw parallel computing. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments, making it highly suitable for hosting databases, running caching servers, and executing memory-intensive enterprise applications.
